skill.name: obsidian-vault
skill.description: Search, create, and organize notes in a local Obsidian vault using filename searches, content searches, wikilinks, and index notes.
skill.summary: Manage a local Obsidian knowledge vault with search, wikilinks, and index-note workflows.
skill.author: mattpocock

security_audit.summary: The static external-command findings are partially true positives because the skill tells agents to use shell tools such as find and grep against a local vault. I did not find malicious intent, network exfiltration, prompt injection, or weak cryptography; the high weak-cryptography alert at SKILL.md line 3 is a false positive from natural-language metadata.

medium_findings.title: Local Vault Filesystem Access
medium_findings.description: The skill directs agents to search and manage notes at a fixed Obsidian vault path. This is core functionality, but it can expose private note contents and create or modify local files if used without user confirmation.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file contains a fixed local vault path and commands that search markdown files there. The behavior is intentional and useful, but it clearly touches potentially private local notes.

medium_findings.title: External Command Guidance for Note Search
medium_findings.description: The skill documents find and grep commands for searching filenames, note contents, backlinks, and index notes. The paths are quoted and no command injection pattern is evident, but agents may execute shell commands over user data.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The shell commands are explicit and easy to verify. Risk is moderated because the examples use hardcoded quoted paths and placeholders rather than direct unsanitized user input.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alert Dismissed
low_findings.description: The static high-risk weak cryptography alert points to the skill description metadata. I found no cryptographic algorithm, hashing code, encryption code, or security-sensitive crypto usage in that line.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is plain descriptive text about Obsidian note workflows. There is no semantic evidence of a weak cryptographic algorithm.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Commands Over Local Markdown Files
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes shell command examples using find and grep against a local Obsidian vault. These are not malicious, but they are security-relevant because they access local files outside a project workspace.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are present in fenced bash blocks and directly reference the local vault. I did not find network transfer, obfuscation, or destructive command usage.

content.value_statement: Large Obsidian vaults become hard to search, link, and maintain consistently. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code concrete workflows for finding notes, creating wikilinks, and maintaining index notes.
actual_capabilities: Searches a configured local Obsidian vault by markdown filename.
Searches note contents with grep-style workflows.
Creates new notes using title case file naming guidance.
Adds Obsidian wikilinks to related notes.
Finds backlinks by searching for existing wikilink references.
Finds index notes using the vault naming convention.
limitations: Assumes the vault is located at the path documented in SKILL.md.
Does not define a sync, backup, or conflict-resolution workflow.
Does not validate note content against a formal schema.
Requires local filesystem access to search or modify the vault.

use_cases.title: Find Existing Research Notes
use_cases.description: Search filenames and note contents before creating duplicate research material.

use_cases.title: Create Linked Learning Notes
use_cases.description: Draft new title-case notes and add relevant wikilinks to related concepts.

use_cases.title: Maintain Index Notes
use_cases.description: Locate index notes and update them with links to related vault entries.

prompt_templates.title: Search for a Note
prompt_templates.prompt: Search my Obsidian vault for notes about {topic}. Report matching note titles and the strongest content matches.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Linked Note
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a new Obsidian note titled {note_title}. Use title case, write a concise learning note, and add wikilinks to related notes.

prompt_templates.title: Find Backlinks
prompt_templates.prompt: Find notes that link to [[{note_title}]]. Summarize the relationship between those notes and the target note.

prompt_templates.title: Update an Index Note
prompt_templates.prompt: Find the best index note for {topic}. Add relevant wikilinks for existing related notes and suggest any missing notes to create.

output_examples.input: Search for notes about retrieval augmented generation.
output_examples.output: Found several matching notes, including a RAG index note and related concept notes. The strongest matches include content about retrieval, embeddings, and evaluation.

output_examples.input: Create a note about prompt routing.
output_examples.output: Created a title-case note with a short explanation, practical examples, and wikilinks to related prompt engineering and system design notes.

output_examples.input: Find backlinks for a note about skills.
output_examples.output: Found notes that reference the target note through wikilinks. Grouped the backlinks by direct references, index entries, and related concept mentions.

best_practices: Confirm the target vault path before searching or creating notes.
Search existing notes before creating a new note.
Use wikilinks and index notes to preserve the vault structure.
anti_patterns: Do not create duplicate notes without checking existing filenames and content.
Do not reorganize the vault into folders when the vault uses flat organization.
Do not expose private note contents unless the user asks for them.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps agents search, create, link, and organize notes in a local Obsidian vault.

faq.question: Does it require a specific vault path?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill documents a specific local vault path and assumes that path exists.

faq.question: Can it create new notes?
faq.answer: Yes. It provides naming and linking guidance for creating new markdown notes.

faq.question: Can it update index notes?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains how to find index notes and use wikilinks to organize related notes.

faq.question: Does it send note contents to the network?
faq.answer: No evidence found. The skill only documents local filesystem search workflows.

faq.question: What should users review before publishing?
faq.answer: Users should review local filesystem access because vault searches may reveal private note contents.
